tools.name: autobot_local_agent_clients
tools.description: lists local SHAFT Autobot CLI clients that do not require SHAFT cloud API keys

tools.name: autobot_local_agent_run
tools.description: routes Ask, Plan, or approved Agent requests to Codex, Claude Code, or Copilot CLI

tools.name: autobot_provider_chat
tools.description: routes Ask or Plan requests to configured SHAFT cloud providers without source mutation

tools.name: autobot_provider_status
tools.description: reports the configured SHAFT cloud provider, model, API-key presence (never the value), and structured-output support for the IntelliJ readiness view

tools.name: browser_accessibility_audit
tools.description: runs a non-asserting axe-core WCAG accessibility audit on the current page and returns the violations found; never fails the call when violations exist; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: browser_aria_snapshot
tools.description: captures an accessible-name-tree aria snapshot (YAML) of the whole page, or of one element when a locator is supplied; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: browser_delete_cookies
tools.description: deletes a cookie by name, or every cookie when name is omitted; dispatches to the active engine; absorbs browser_delete_cookie/browser_delete_all_cookies

tools.name: browser_get_current_url
tools.description: gets current URL; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: browser_get_page_dom
tools.description: returns bounded current-page DOM for locator inspection before element_*; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: browser_get_title
tools.description: gets current page title; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: browser_navigate
tools.description: opens a URL in the active engine's browser session; optional newWindow (default false) opens the URL in a new window instead of navigating the current one, absorbing playwright_browser_new_window's window-opening behavior on the Playwright engine

tools.name: browser_navigate_back
tools.description: navigates back to the previous page; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: browser_navigate_forward
tools.description: navigates forward to the next page; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: browser_network_requests
tools.description: lists the active browser session's observed network transactions (method, URL, status, mimeType, sizes, timestamp; never bodies); optional id narrows the listing to that single transaction and populates its full detail (headers and a truncated, redacted response body preview), absorbing the former browser_network_request tool; requires the DevTools-based network trace capture that is on by default (shaft.trace.enabled and shaft.trace.includeNetwork); not supported on the Playwright engine

tools.name: browser_open_intent
tools.description: opens a URL and returns bounded DOM plus capture-ranked locator candidates for the user intent; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: browser_refresh
tools.description: refreshes the current page; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: browser_route
tools.description: registers a mock network route on the active browser session, matching by optional HTTP method and a URL glob (Playwright-style * and ?) or an exact URL, and returning the given status/body/headers instead of the real network response; returns a route id for reference (individual removal is not supported -- browser_unroute always clears every route)

tools.name: browser_set_window_size
tools.description: sets the browser window to a specific size; optional mode selects custom (default, uses width/height) | maximize | fullscreen, absorbing browser_maximize_window/browser_fullscreen_window; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: browser_storage_state_load
tools.description: loads cookies, localStorage, and sessionStorage from a JSON file into the active browser session; navigate to the target origin first; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: browser_storage_state_save
tools.description: saves the active browser session's cookies, localStorage, and sessionStorage to a JSON file; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: browser_take_screenshot
tools.description: takes a PNG screenshot of the current browser viewport; dispatches to the active engine (web, mobile, or Playwright)

tools.name: browser_unroute
tools.description: clears registered browser mock routes for the active session; routeId is accepted for reference but every route is cleared because SHAFT's network interceptor does not support removing a single rule yet

tools.name: capture_api_generate
tools.description: generates, compiles, and returns copy-paste SHAFT.API code blocks from recorded API transactions

tools.name: capture_api_response_leaves
tools.description: returns classified response-body leaves (stable/volatile/sensitive) per transaction for a recorded session without generating any test source, for a pin-this-path picker; sensitive values are redacted

tools.name: capture_api_start
tools.description: starts SHAFT Capture with API network recording enabled; dispatches to the mobile loopback MITM proxy when a mobile engine is active OR mobilePlatform is explicitly given (the mobile proxy never requires a live Appium/WebDriver session), absorbing mobile_api_record_start (outputPath/mobilePlatform/mobileDeviceLabel are mobile-only and ignored on WEB)

tools.name: capture_api_status
tools.description: returns SHAFT Capture session status including network transaction count and recent endpoints; dispatches to the mobile loopback MITM proxy when a mobile engine is active, or when a standalone mobile API session (started with no active engine) is already running

tools.name: capture_api_stop
tools.description: stops SHAFT API Capture with the same single-session lock guarantee as capture_stop; dispatches to the mobile loopback MITM proxy when a mobile engine is active, or when a standalone mobile API session (started with no active engine) is already running

tools.name: capture_api_transactions
tools.description: returns captured network transactions without bodies or sensitive headers; supports filtering asset noise on WEB; dispatches to the mobile loopback MITM proxy's transactions when a mobile engine is active, or when a standalone mobile API session is already running (includeAssets/excludePattern are WEB-only there)

tools.name: capture_backend_comparison
tools.description: compares WebDriver and Playwright Capture code-block outputs without editing source

tools.name: capture_checkpoint
tools.description: records a USER_MARKER, ASSERTION, PAGE_TRANSITION, RECOVERY, FLOW_START, or FLOW_END checkpoint

tools.name: capture_code_blocks
tools.description: generates a Java full-class snippet plus agent guidance for repo-aware insertion from a persisted recording JSON (sessionPath); works on any recording file, no active capture session required; optional backend (web|playwright|mobile) selects the codegen target, defaulting to the active engine (absorbs playwright_capture_code_blocks/mobile_recording_code_blocks)

params.name: backend
params.description: codegen target: web (default) | playwright | mobile; blank infers from the active engine

params.name: className
params.description: Java class name for the generated class; defaults to RecordedFlowTest

params.name: driverVariableName
params.description: Java driver variable name used in extracted snippets; defaults to driver

params.name: outputDirectory
params.description: generated project root inside the MCP workspace; defaults to generated-tests under the workspace

params.name: overwrite
params.description: overwrite an existing generated file; defaults to false

params.name: packageName
params.description: Java package for the generated class; defaults to tests.generated

params.name: sessionPath
params.description: persisted Capture JSON path inside the MCP workspace; blank uses the most recently modified recording under recordings/

tools.name: capture_codegen_features
tools.description: returns Playwright codegen features and how SHAFT Capture/MCP maps each feature

tools.name: capture_evidence_pack
tools.description: returns a local manifest of Capture source, report, review UI, screenshots, and checks

tools.name: capture_generate_replay
tools.description: generates, compiles, optionally replays, and returns copy-paste SHAFT code blocks from a persisted recording JSON (sessionPath); works on any recording file, no active capture session required; optional backend (web|playwright|mobile) selects the codegen target, defaulting to the active engine (absorbs playwright_capture_generate_replay/playwright_replay_recording/mobile_replay_recording)

params.name: backend
params.description: codegen target: web (default) | playwright | mobile; blank infers from the active engine

params.name: sessionPath
params.description: persisted Capture JSON path inside the MCP workspace; blank uses the most recently modified recording under recordings/

tools.name: capture_pick_locator
tools.description: ranks caller-supplied locator candidates for a picked element and returns a copy-paste SHAFT.GUI.Locator snippet

tools.name: capture_record_at_target_code_blocks
tools.description: generates focused Capture snippets for insertion at an existing Java source anchor; optional backend (web|playwright|mobile) selects the codegen target, defaulting to the active engine (absorbs playwright_record_at_target_code_blocks/mobile_record_at_target_code_blocks)

params.name: backend
params.description: codegen target: web (default) | playwright | mobile; blank infers from the active engine

params.name: className
params.description: Java class name for the generated class; defaults to RecordedFlowTest

params.name: driverVariableName
params.description: Java driver variable name used in extracted snippets; defaults to driver

params.name: outputDirectory
params.description: generated project root inside the MCP workspace; defaults to generated-tests under the workspace

params.name: overwrite
params.description: overwrite an existing generated file; defaults to false

params.name: packageName
params.description: Java package for the generated class; defaults to tests.generated

params.name: sessionPath
params.description: persisted Capture JSON path inside the MCP workspace; blank uses the most recently modified recording under recordings/

tools.name: capture_set_mode
tools.description: reads or toggles the active recorder's live authoring mode: record (default) or inspect

tools.name: capture_start
tools.description: starts a recording, dispatching to the active engine: WEB (or no active session) always launches its own privacy-safe SHAFT-managed browser via CDP capture, and while it is active, element_* tools drive the recorded browser directly (no driver_initialize needed); PLAYWRIGHT/mobile sessions instead record actions on the already-active driver (targetUrl/browser/headless are WEB-only; sessionGoal names the generated test on WEB, or is used as the recorder's mode label on PLAYWRIGHT/mobile); optional nested codegenOptions replaces the flat targetUrl/browser/outputPath/headless/sessionGoal args with the full Playwright-codegen-compatible request (viewport, geolocation, HAR, proxy, etc.), absorbing the former capture_start_codegen tool -- WEB/NONE only

params.name: browser
params.description: Chrome or Edge; blank selects Chrome; WEB/NONE only

params.name: codegenOptions
params.description: full Playwright-codegen-compatible request (viewport, geolocation, HAR, proxy, API capture, etc.); when supplied, its own fields replace targetUrl/browser/outputPath/headless/sessionGoal entirely; WEB/NONE only, absorbs capture_start_codegen

params.name: headless
params.description: whether to launch without a visible window; blank/omitted defaults to headless per repo policy; WEB/NONE only

params.name: outputPath
params.description: recording/capture JSON path; blank selects a timestamped recording

params.name: sessionGoal
params.description: optional user intent for the journey; drives generated test class and method names on WEB, or the recorder's mode label on PLAYWRIGHT/mobile

params.name: targetUrl
params.description: initial http, https, or file URL; blank opens an empty browser; WEB/NONE only

tools.name: capture_status
tools.description: returns recorder status for the active engine: WEB (or no active session) returns SHAFT Capture session, browser, URL, event count, pending debounced-signal count, warnings, and output status (when no session is active the warnings list persisted recordings that can be turned into code without a live session); PLAYWRIGHT/mobile return that engine's own recorder status; the union's engine field names which is populated

tools.name: capture_step_delete
tools.description: deletes a recorded step by its stable stepId from the active Playwright or mobile recording; returns an actionable error naming the active engine for a WEB CDP capture_start session, whose recording format has no step editor

tools.name: capture_step_reorder
tools.description: moves a recorded step up or down by its stable stepId (direction: up|down) within the active Playwright or mobile recording; returns an actionable error naming the active engine for a WEB CDP capture_start session, whose recording format has no step editor

tools.name: capture_stop
tools.description: stops the active recording for the active engine; after WEB reaches COMPLETED, show outputPath and generate replay-proven code by passing it as sessionPath to capture_generate_replay (or capture_code_blocks for a faster, unproven draft); the union's engine field names which recorder was stopped

tools.name: capture_target_candidates
tools.description: suggests existing Java Page Object or test targets for Capture record-at-target insertion

tools.name: doctor_analyze_failed_allure
tools.description: analyzes failed Allure results and returns deterministic actions plus copy-paste code blocks; accepts allure-results directories, individual *-result.json files, or a SHAFT single-file AllureReport.html; when allureResultPaths is empty, automatically analyzes the newest such evidence found in the workspace; optional backend (web|playwright, defaults to web) selects the generated remediation snippets' engine, absorbing playwright_doctor_analyze_failed_allure

tools.name: doctor_analyze_trace
tools.description: analyzes a persisted SHAFT trace and returns deterministic Doctor remediation guidance

tools.name: doctor_propose_healed_locator
tools.description: maps a verified SHAFT Heal report to one reviewable locator patch without editing source or publishing

tools.name: doctor_suggest_fix
tools.description: returns copy-paste remediation code blocks from an existing SHAFT Doctor report; optional backend (web|playwright, defaults to web) selects the generated snippets' engine, absorbing playwright_doctor_suggest_fix

tools.name: driver_initialize
tools.description: launches browser, Playwright, or a mobile session; optional engine selects web (default) | playwright | mobile_native | mobile_web; for mobile engines, optional nested mobileOptions carries native/web-emulation parameters, absorbing mobile_initialize_native/mobile_initialize_web_emulation

params.name: targetBrowser
params.description: browser to launch on the WEB engine; blank/omitted defers to the configured targetBrowserName property (default chrome); PLAYWRIGHT/MOBILE engines resolve their own default independently

tools.name: driver_quit
tools.description: closes whichever engine session (web, mobile, or Playwright) is currently active

tools.name: element_clear
tools.description: clears text from an element; dispatches to the active engine and records the step when a mobile or Playwright recording is active

tools.name: element_click
tools.description: clicks an element; optional mode selects single (default) | double | long; dispatches to the active engine and records the step when a mobile or Playwright recording is active

tools.name: element_drag_and_drop
tools.description: drags and drops an element from source to a target locator, or by offsetX/offsetY when no target locator is given; dispatches to the active engine and records the step when a Playwright recording is active

tools.name: element_hover
tools.description: hovers over an element; dispatches to the active engine and records the step when a Playwright recording is active

tools.name: element_is_displayed
tools.description: checks if an element is displayed; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: element_is_enabled
tools.description: checks if an element is enabled; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: element_is_selected
tools.description: checks if an element is selected; dispatches to the active engine

tools.name: element_type
tools.description: types text into an element; optional append (default false) appends instead of clearing first, optional clear (default true; false behaves like append=true) controls whether the field is cleared before typing; dispatches to the active engine and records the step when a mobile or Playwright recording is active

tools.name: element_upload_file
tools.description: drops file to an element to upload; dispatches to the active engine and records the step when a Playwright recording is active

tools.name: generate_test_report
tools.description: generates a test report for the current session

tools.name: healer_run_failed_test
tools.description: reruns a failing SHAFT/Selenium test, analyzes fresh Allure evidence, and returns review-only fixes plus agent replay guidance; optional backend (web|playwright, defaults to web) selects the generated snippets' engine, absorbing playwright_healer_run_failed_test

tools.name: mobile_activate_app
tools.description: activates an installed app by Android package or iOS bundle id

tools.name: mobile_background_app
tools.description: sends the active mobile app to the background

tools.name: mobile_get_accessibility_tree
tools.description: gets the current Appium native accessibility XML tree or mobile web source

tools.name: mobile_get_contexts
tools.description: gets Appium contexts plus current native XML or web DOM source

tools.name: mobile_hide_keyboard
tools.description: hides the native mobile keyboard

tools.name: mobile_inspector_record_start
tools.description: prepares and starts a wrapped Appium Inspector recording session in one call, absorbing mobile_inspector_record_prepare

tools.name: mobile_inspector_record_status
tools.description: returns the wrapped Appium Inspector recording status; optional action (pause|resume|checkpoint|stop|discard) performs that control first, absorbing mobile_inspector_record_control

tools.name: mobile_inspector_record_stop
tools.description: stops a wrapped Appium Inspector recording and returns generated replay code

tools.name: mobile_keyboard_key
tools.description: sends a native keyboard action such as DONE, SEARCH, GO, NEXT, or SEND

tools.name: mobile_rotate
tools.description: rotates the mobile device to PORTRAIT or LANDSCAPE

tools.name: mobile_swipe
tools.description: swipes on the mobile screen; dispatches on whichever optional params are supplied -- text (swipe to text) | locator+offsetX/offsetY (swipe by offset) | locator alone (swipe element into view, optional direction) | startX/startY/endX/endY (coordinate escape hatch); absorbs mobile_swipe_by_offset/mobile_swipe_coordinates/mobile_swipe_element_into_view/mobile_swipe_text_into_view

tools.name: mobile_switch_context
tools.description: switches Appium context, for example NATIVE_APP or WEBVIEW_*

tools.name: mobile_tap_coordinates
tools.description: fallback-only: taps viewport coordinates only after locator-based element_click cannot be used

tools.name: mobile_toolchain_status
tools.description: checks local Appium, Inspector, adb, emulator, and SDK tooling status with repair diagnostics

tools.name: report_merge_shards
tools.description: merges N per-shard Allure/trace/doctor-intelligence blobs into one Allure result set plus a flaky-clustering speedboard HTML

tools.name: shaft_coding_partner_diff
tools.description: produces a preview-only unified diff that inserts reviewed SHAFT code blocks into an existing Java target at a chosen method or textual anchor; never writes files

tools.name: shaft_coding_partner_plan
tools.description: plans a repository-aware SHAFT IntelliJ coding-partner workflow using current source, selected text, existing page objects/tests, capture evidence, and verification guidance

tools.name: shaft_guide_search
tools.description: searches the live official SHAFT user guide before writing SHAFT tests, page objects, locators, API, GUI, CLI, mobile, troubleshooting, or best-practice code

tools.name: shaft_project_create
tools.description: creates a new SHAFT Maven project from the same examples and rules used by the guide generator

tools.name: shaft_project_init_agents
tools.description: scaffolds SHAFT agent/skill bridge files and a merge-me AGENTS.md guidance file into an existing test repo for a coding-agent loop (claude, codex, opencode, vscode), without overwriting files that already exist unless overwrite=true

tools.name: shaft_project_upgrade
tools.description: runs the existing SHAFT modular project upgrader script against the current Java project

tools.name: test_automation_scenarios
tools.description: returns SHAFT MCP usage scenarios, agent actions, design patterns, guardrails, and completion criteria for API, GUI, mobile, capture, Doctor, Heal, reporting, and CI work

tools.name: test_code_guardrails_check
tools.description: checks generated SHAFT test code for lexical anti-patterns such as sleeps, brittle locators, raw driver calls, headed setup, direct system properties, and obvious secrets

tools.name: test_plan_explore
tools.description: crawls the active SHAFT browser session breadth-first from a URL, discovering forms, primary navigation actions, and same-origin links, and writes one deterministic Markdown test plan per discovered flow into specs/ (no AI calls)

tools.name: trace_latest
tools.description: returns recent persisted SHAFT trace indexes from target/shaft-traces

tools.name: trace_open_viewer
tools.description: resolves (extracting from the trace ZIP if needed) the offline SHAFT Trace Report HTML viewer for a persisted trace

tools.name: trace_read
tools.description: returns redacted SHAFT trace JSON from a trace path with explicit output bounds

tools.name: trace_summarize
tools.description: returns a deterministic summary of a persisted SHAFT trace without AI

tools.name: verify_run_focused
tools.description: runs a guarded, tokenized Maven verification command (compile, test-compile, test, verify, package) headlessly inside the MCP workspace and returns a bounded pass/fail summary; release and deploy goals are rejected
